answers: Aluminium does not belong because it is the only metal.
Pine does not belong because it is the only natural solid timber.
MDF does not belong because it is made from wood fibres.
Plywood does not belong because it is made from layered veneers.
Sandpaper does not belong because it is an abrasive rather than a cutting or striking tool.
Hammer does not belong because it does not cut material.
Saw does not belong because it removes material by teeth rather than impact or abrasion.
Rivet does not belong because it is a metal fastening method.
Mortise and tenon does not belong because it is a traditional formed joint rather than a loose inserted connector.
Biscuit does not belong because it usually relies on swelling compressed beech.
Recipe does not belong because it is not a drawing or drawing view.
Isometric does not belong because it is a pictorial view, while plan and elevation are orthographic views.
Photosynthesis does not belong because it is not part of a manufacturing process.
Joining does not belong because it usually happens after shaping steps like marking out and cutting.
Glue does not belong because it is an adhesive rather than a mechanical fastener.
Bolt does not belong because it is usually used with a nut.
Nail does not belong because it has no thread.
Jandal does not belong because it is not suitable workshop footwear.
Apron does not belong because it is not always required PPE in the same way eye and hearing protection can be.
Plywood does not belong because it is not a metal.
Aluminium does not belong because it is much lighter than the others.
Copper does not belong because it is commonly used for electrical conductivity.
Hot glue gun does not belong because it is not a measuring or checking tool.
Try square does not belong because it is mainly for checking 90 degrees rather than measuring length.
MDF does not belong because it is an engineered board, not solid timber.
Rimu does not belong because it is a native timber.
Pine does not belong because it is usually plantation-grown and softer than many other timbers.
Sleeping does not belong because it is not a manufacturing process.
Laminating does not belong because it joins layers rather than forming liquid or softened material in a mould.
Wallpaper does not belong because it is not a mechanism.
Lever does not belong because it is a simple machine that does not rely on rotational transmission like gears and pulleys.
